summary: Gruber reports with undisguised glee that Special Counsel Jack Smith obtained Donald Trump's Twitter direct messages, including deleted ones, as part of the investigation into the aftermath of the 2020 election. The unsealed court transcripts reveal Trump had 'many' DMs despite his well-known aversion to written communications to avoid leaving evidence trails. Twitter/X was held in contempt and fined $350,000 for missing a deadline and for taking unprecedented steps to give Trump advance notice of the search warrant. Judge Howell repeatedly questioned whether Elon Musk's Twitter was trying to 'cozy up' to Trump. Gruber uses this as validation of his earlier speculation that nothing on Twitter is truly private.
key_points: Trump used Twitter DMs extensively despite his famous aversion to written communications and leaving paper trails
Twitter retained both deleted and non-deleted DMs, confirming that 'deleted' messages are merely hidden, not destroyed
Twitter was held in contempt and fined $350,000 for trying to give Trump advance notice of the search warrant, something it had never done for other users
Judge Howell repeatedly questioned whether Musk's Twitter was taking extraordinary steps to protect Trump specifically
The search warrant covered not just DMs but also search history, location data, IP addresses, and draft posts from October 2020 to January 2021
money_quotes: I am slightly surprised that Trump — famously averse not just to using email and text messages, but even to his own lawyers taking written notes in meetings, so as not to leave a chain of evidence for his lifelong criminal activity — would use, of all things, the infamously unencrypted direct messaging feature on Twitter.
To be clear, this is a pleasant surprise.
Nothing you do on Twitter is private. Not your DMs, not your 'deleted' DMs, not your searches, not your location, not your draft posts.
Elon Musk comes out of this looking like he'd happily fellate Trump.
Hope you stocked up on popcorn, like I did, for indictment season.
I am not at all surprised that 'deleted' DMs are not in fact deleted, but rather hidden.
Is it because the new CEO wants to cozy up with the former president?
key_insight: Trump's lifelong strategy of avoiding written communications to evade accountability was undone by his own use of Twitter DMs, which the platform retained even after deletion.
